customer_promise: An admin pastes messy commercial-invoice requirements and within one demo session sees compiled Document AI configuration, a clear capability assessment, a deterministic DAEJOO extraction, a readiness decision with business reasons, agentic clarification prompts for missed fields, admin-grade improvement recommendations grounded in observed corrections, and a separate internal flywheel view — all without telling the customer their requirement is "unsupported," without auto-lowering a threshold to fake a win, and without leaking internal roadmap gaps into any customer surface.
negative_contract: Never display "unsupported" in the Customer Workspace; never recommend lowering a confidence threshold as the demo action; never surface raw prompts, raw model output, or roadmap-candidate signals (including the DAEJOO material-disposal phrase) on a customer surface; never call live OCR or live field extraction in v1; never mark a document Ready when any required field is below auto-confirm or when a workaround rule is unconfirmed for the supplier; never auto-promote a single correction into a ProductSignal without governance gating; never read the DAEJOO PDF from ~/Downloads at runtime (canonical path is app/assets/daejoo-invoice.pdf); never hide an AI Core agent failure behind a canned fallback (use ClarificationRequest + QualityMetric instead).
customer: A Document AI administrator configuring commercial-invoice processing on behalf of an enterprise AP/logistics team, whose downstream consumer is an internal operator triaging extraction results.
problem: Administrators translate prose business requirements into Document AI schema, prompts, regex, and thresholds by hand, producing silent capability mismatches that surface only as downstream STP failures or operator overrides.
solution_fit: A live AI Core compile step turns intent into a single CompiledConfiguration artifact, paired with a deterministic DAEJOO extraction and live agentic capability, readiness, clarification, admin-recommendation, and signal-governance steps that make capability gaps visible without ever saying "unsupported."
behavior_change: Administrators stop hand-editing prompts and thresholds and instead review readiness in business language, confirm or correct fields through the clarification loop, and accept or defer admin recommendations that always cite the correction or signal that produced them.
advantage: A three-surface separation (Customer / Admin / Internal) with a governed product-signal flywheel that converts hidden capability gaps into roadmap evidence without ever exposing those gaps to the customer — a structural separation competitors that auto-tune thresholds or surface "unsupported" labels cannot copy without rebuilding their control plane.
tam_roi: Every enterprise Document AI tenant configuring non-trivial document types faces the same silent-mismatch tax; a control layer that compounds corrections into product roadmap evidence amortises one administrator's clarification work across every future tenant configuring the same document type.
constraints: v1 runs on a single demo laptop with no live OCR, no database, no auth, no shared runtime, the DAEJOO PDF as the only anchor document, deterministic mock extraction, live SAP AI Core for every agent step, and binary Vitest evals — all eval families must pass and the full walkthrough must complete in under five minutes.
